key_highlights: Markets surged to new highs on hopes of a US-Iran peace deal, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq hitting records.
Tech earnings remain robust, with Sandisk up nearly 500% in 2026, CoreWeave, Arm, and DoorDash all beating estimates.
Oil prices plunged on ceasefire prospects, but jet fuel costs remain elevated, pressuring airlines like British Airways and Air India.
Gold and silver rallied as inflation fears eased on the Iran truce, while the DOJ investigates suspicious oil trades ahead of war news.
Geopolitical tensions remain high with a reported US bombing of an Iranian coastal city, but Trump paused Hormuz operations citing progress.
Consumer staples face headwinds: food prices hit three-year highs, gas prices climb, and Spirit Airlines suffered a shock shutdown.
Defense and energy transition themes persist: Rheinmetall sees strong demand, Einride pushes electric autonomous freight, and Airbus won a $19bn Canadian order.
Central banks are diversifying reserves into Malaysia bonds, and private credit funds are tapping wealthy clients.

sectors.trend: Strong earnings momentum across data center, AI, and CPU segments. Sandisk (data center storage) up ~500% YTD, CoreWeave (cloud/AI) beat Q1 estimates, Arm carved a lucrative CPU path, and IonQ doubled down on Nvidia comparisons despite post-earnings dip. AMD held annual meeting.
sectors.implications: AI and data center infrastructure remain the primary growth engine. Valuations are stretched but fundamentals support continued inflows. Short squeezes possible (e.g., OKLO).

sectors.trend: Oil prices dropped sharply on US-Iran ceasefire hopes, but jet fuel costs remain high. The Permian Basin is highlighted as a strategic asset. Flowco Holdings reported Q1 earnings. SolarEdge had a Q1 call.
sectors.implications: Short-term oil volatility is high; any breakdown in talks could spike prices. Airlines and transport are squeezed by fuel costs. Energy transition (electric autonomous freight) gains attention.

sectors.trend: Rheinmetall CEO cited strong demand from German defense spending. Airbus won a $19bn order from Canada. War risks continue to drive defense budgets.
sectors.implications: Defense stocks remain supported by geopolitical tensions and increased NATO spending. Aerospace benefits from commercial orders but faces fuel cost headwinds.

sectors.trend: Expedia beat on Middle East resilience and World Cup outlook. Disney surged on streaming profits. DoorDash rallied on earnings. Spirit Airlines apologized after a shock shutdown. British Airways owner sees profit hit from fuel costs. Air India cutting costs. Food prices at three-year highs.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary is mixed: travel demand resilient but fuel costs and inflation pressure margins. Food inflation hurts low-income consumers. Streaming profitability is a bright spot.

sectors.trend: Insulet, Novavax, Bioventus, LivaNova, Azenta, BeOne Medicines all reported earnings. Novavax Q1 call highlights were noted. Cruise ship hantavirus outbreak raises travel health risks.
sectors.implications: Healthcare earnings mixed; Novavax may see renewed interest if vaccine demand persists. Biotech remains event-driven.

sectors.trend: Victor Khosla warned of 'fat-tail' risk and fragile credit markets. Palmer Square Capital BDC reported. UniCredit plan puts revenue at risk. Granite Asia taps DBS wealthy for private credit. Central banks boost Malaysia bond holdings.
sectors.implications: Credit markets are tight; private credit continues to grow. Central bank diversification into emerging market bonds is a positive signal. Earnings reporting frequency proposal (twice a year) could reduce transparency.

sectors.trend: Owens Corning reported Q1 and Q4 2025 earnings. Tips on rent vs. buy decisions. Nextdoor stock at inflection point.
sectors.implications: Housing market remains mixed; higher rates and inflation impact affordability. Building materials companies face cost pressures.

sectors.trend: Wayve CEO discussed AI transforming self-driving cars. Waymo's Thirumalai on future of AVs. Vay CEO plans to replace private car ownership. Tesla Model Y meets new US driver assistance safety benchmark. Stellantis deal gives Leapmotor a plant in Europe.
sectors.implications: Autonomous driving is progressing but commercialization remains gradual. Regulatory benchmarks (Tesla) and partnerships (Stellantis-Leapmotor) are key catalysts.

key_themes: US-Iran peace deal hopes driving risk-on sentiment and oil price decline
Robust tech earnings, especially in AI/data center infrastructure
Geopolitical risk remains elevated despite ceasefire progress
Inflation pressures persist in food and fuel, but gold rally suggests easing fears
Central bank diversification into emerging market bonds
Private credit expansion and credit market fragility warnings
Defense spending increases in Europe and globally
Autonomous vehicle and AI regulatory milestones
Airlines and transport squeezed by fuel costs and war disruptions
Consumer staples inflation and travel demand divergence

risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify into gold, defensive sectors, and energy hedges. Monitor ceasefire talks closely.
Reduce exposure to airlines and transport; consider long positions in energy producers or hedges via futures/options.
Avoid high-yield credit; favor investment-grade bonds and private credit with strong covenants. Monitor DOJ investigation into oil trades.
Overweight consumer staples with pricing power; underweight discretionary. Consider TIPS or commodity ETFs.
Monitor proposal; if implemented, could reduce transparency and increase information asymmetry.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Central bank digital currency / stablecoin risks
risk_assessment.mitigation: Lagarde's comments highlight potential banking disintermediation; monitor regulatory developments.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Travel disruption (cruise ship virus, airline shutdowns)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid concentrated positions in travel; diversify across subsectors.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase exposure to AI/data center infrastructure stocks (e.g., Sandisk, CoreWeave, Arm) on pullbacks.
opportunities.rationale: Earnings momentum and structural demand remain strong; valuations are high but justified by growth.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy gold and silver on dips as a hedge against geopolitical risk and inflation.
opportunities.rationale: Prices surged on truce hopes but remain supported by central bank buying and real rate environment.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider long positions in defense stocks (e.g., Rheinmetall) given sustained NATO spending.
opportunities.rationale: German defense budget increase and global tensions support multi-year demand.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in electric autonomous freight companies (e.g., Einride) for long-term transport disruption.
opportunities.rationale: Fuel cost pressures and regulatory push for decarbonization accelerate adoption.

opportunities.recommendation: Add to positions in streaming leaders (Disney, DoorDash) on earnings strength.
opportunities.rationale: Streaming profitability and delivery demand remain resilient despite macro headwinds.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to airlines and travel intermediaries (British Airways, Air India, Spirit) due to fuel cost and geopolitical risks.
defensive_moves.rationale: Jet fuel prices spiking and war disruptions hurt margins; demand may soften if conflict escalates.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge oil price exposure via put options or short energy ETFs if ceasefire holds.
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil could drop further if Iran deal is finalized; but any breakdown could spike prices, so use options.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Shift a portion of fixed income into short-duration investment-grade bonds or private credit funds.
defensive_moves.rationale: Credit markets are fragile; short duration reduces interest rate risk and default risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary stocks with high fuel cost sensitivity.
defensive_moves.rationale: Food and gas price inflation squeeze lower-income consumers; Dine Brands and similar may underperform.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets are likely to remain volatile but biased upward if US-Iran ceasefire holds. Tech earnings momentum and falling oil prices support risk-on sentiment. However, any breakdown in talks or DOJ investigation findings could trigger sharp reversals. Focus on earnings reports from McDonald's and Airbnb.
market_outlook.long_term: Structural trends in AI, defense, and energy transition remain intact. Inflation may moderate if oil stays lower, but food prices and wage pressures persist. Central bank diversification into emerging markets and private credit will continue. Geopolitical risks are elevated but priced in; a durable peace could unlock significant upside.
key_catalysts: US-Iran ceasefire deal finalization and Hormuz navigation resumption
DOJ investigation outcomes on suspicious oil trades
Fed policy signals amid inflation and growth data
Q2 earnings season guidance from tech and consumer sectors
German industrial production and Eurozone economic data
Canadian federal election results and energy policy
World Cup 2026 travel demand boost for Middle East
Bitcoin miner data center deals and crypto regulatory moves
watch_list: Oil prices (WTI, Brent) and jet fuel spreads
US dollar index and emerging market bond yields
Malaysia bond holdings by global central banks
Consumer price indices for food and energy
